steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a difference in umami intensity: winged kelp has moderate umami while arame has higher umami. The correct choice must contrast these two intensities clearly.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — While winged kelp and arame contain umami flavor, umami can also be found in ham and mushrooms.: mentions that winged kelp and arame both contain umami, then pivots to comparing ham and mushrooms — it groups the two seaweeds together rather than contrasting their intensity levels. ✗
  • Choice B — Some types of brown seaweed, like winged kelp and arame, trigger umami flavor in human taste buds.: states that both seaweeds trigger umami responses, again treating them as equivalent rather than contrasting winged kelp's moderate umami with arame's higher umami. ✗
  • Choice C — Although winged kelp and arame are types of brown seaweed, the latter's umami flavor is more intense.: explicitly states that arame's umami is more intense than winged kelp's, directly emphasizing the difference the student needs. ✓
  • Choice D — Winged kelp is a type of brown seaweed, but so is arame.: only notes that both winged kelp and arame are brown seaweeds — a botanical classification, not a comparison of umami intensities. ✗
That is the correct answer.
